data.narrative:
Isar Aerospace is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company seeking valuation following $400M+ in total capital raised and a current post-money valuation exceeding $1.0B. The peer universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies at similar development stages, with 4 directly comparable Series C peers: Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), and Skyryse ($1.1B).

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression anchors on the $1.0B last post-money valuation and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring using the 6-ticker sector median P/S of 64.2x is not applicable given the absence of disclosed revenue data.

The triangulated analysis yields a low of $1.4B, median of $1.7B, and high of $2.0B. Key uncertainties center on revenue trajectory and manufacturing scale relative to peer benchmarks, both critical to frontier-stage space companies. The tight convergence between stage-progression and peer-median methods supports the $1.7B midpoint, though wide peer dispersion ($1.1B–$2.5B) reflects material variance in underlying business maturity and near-term revenue visibility.
